information_gap: The prior CT in the ED was a preliminary read showing 'normal pancreas' but the full picture of the abdomen — including the cholecystostomy tube position, gallbladder status, bowel, mesentery, and any new fluid collections or abscess — has not been formally characterized in the context of this acute worsening, elevated lipase/amylase, neutrophilia, low-grade fever, and recent Serratia sepsis
expected_finding: The physician is hoping to identify or exclude: (1) acute pancreatitis or peripancreatic inflammation not captured on prelim read, (2) cholecystostomy tube complication (dislodgement, pericholecystic abscess, biloma), (3) mesenteric ischemia or vasculitis-related bowel wall thickening given SLE history, (4) new intra-abdominal fluid collection or abscess as source of fever and neutrophilia, (5) bowel obstruction given one-week constipation
